item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read every word. Worth every moment. Written like a scp document should be, about something the Foundation would actually deal with.
Thank [redacted] for authors like you writing articles like this.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have an unsourced image within your article. Please source this image by replying to the sent PM and/or this post within 48 hours or they will have to be deleted. Also, make sure the image complies with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license by following our image use policy, otherwise it will have to be deleted.

48 hours have elapsed, and thus, the image(s) have been removed. Please PM with the source if you wish to have them restored or if you have any questions. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's competently written (somewhat overlong as others have pointed out, but not painfully so), but what brings it down is that at many points it reads like something you've read before: the cult, the overjoyous logs about said cult, the addictive close connection. Terrible? No. But the issues pile up and one is forced to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find this one rather… dry. The experiment logs aren't adding much to the mystery really. There is no hook, the story isn't going anywhere, the one thing that's interesting is the "Teacher", and why they decided to make the others commit suicide is not really hinted at.
There should have been more efforts put into the development of this "Teacher" character imo, since the effects of the braclet themselves aren't that interesting. Instead of using the experiment logs to add to its effects (which imo are already evident from the descriptions), it should have gone into how the "Teacher" would have been affected when their paired mates all suddenly die.
I'm not saying the mystery should have been written out, but more depth to the purpose of the suicide would certainly have been more interesting than what the article is right now. No vote for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is seriously overwritten, taking several paragraphs of exposition on its history and mundane detail on the object s themselves before even detailling its anomalous effects.

It's also entirely unfocused between the personal story of the D-Class incident and the generic cult background; there doesn't seem to be any clear aim for the piece. It doesn't feel coherent, and I'm not sure what's to be my takeaway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty interesting. Didn't go where I thought it would, but the solid writing had me hooked. It's a generous +1 for me.

I have two complaints:

+ show block
– hide block

Exiting effective range severs the connection until such a time when the effective range extends far enough.

Not sure what this means.

Time spent outside of the connection does not contribute to alterations in mental state; when outside of effective range, SCP-4037 is considered not to exhibit its primary anomalous properties in practice.

This is unneccessary, as it's already established that the connection ceases once the wristbands are removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a bit long, but I did enjoy it throughout. I'd recommend giving it a proofread to cut down on unnecessary verbosity.

+1
